note_data.title: Waves (AS7)
keyPoints: **Progressive waves** transfer energy without transferring matter from one point to another
**Transverse waves** have oscillations perpendicular to the direction of energy transfer (e.g. light, radio waves)
**Longitudinal waves** have oscillations parallel to the direction of energy transfer (e.g. sound waves)
Wave speed v = fλ where f is frequency and λ is wavelength
**Doppler effect** occurs when there is relative motion between source and observer, causing change in observed frequency
For approaching source: observed frequency increases, for receding source: observed frequency decreases
**Intensity** is power per unit area perpendicular to the direction of energy transfer
Intensity follows inverse square law: I ∝ 1/r² for point sources
**Amplitude** is maximum displacement from equilibrium position
**Phase difference** is measured in radians or degrees, with 2π radians = 360° = one complete wavelength

definitions.definition: A wave that transfers energy from one point to another without transferring matter
The distance between two adjacent points on a wave that are in phase
The number of complete oscillations per unit time
The power incident normally per unit area
The change in observed frequency due to relative motion between source and observer

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give precise definition that matches mark scheme exactly
Show all working clearly with correct units and significant figures

modelAnswer.question: A sound wave has frequency 440 Hz and wavelength 0.75 m. Calculate the speed of sound and explain the Doppler effect when an ambulance approaches an observer. [5 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: Speed calculation: v = fλ = 440 × 0.75 = 330 m/s. The Doppler effect occurs because there is relative motion between the ambulance (source) and observer. As the ambulance approaches, the observed frequency increases because the sound waves are compressed, resulting in a higher pitch. When receding, frequency decreases as waves are stretched, giving lower pitch.
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Correct calculation v = 330 m/s with unit
Mark 3: States Doppler effect due to relative motion

commonMistakes: Never confuse wavelength with amplitude - wavelength is distance between identical points, amplitude is maximum displacement
Students often forget that intensity is POWER per unit area, not energy per unit area
Don't say 'waves carry matter' - waves transfer energy without transferring matter
For Doppler effect, frequency changes but wave speed in the medium remains constant
Intensity calculations: remember to use perpendicular area, not total surface area
examinerTips: Wave questions appear in almost every Paper 2 - master the basic definitions
Always include units in calculations and check significant figures
For Doppler questions, clearly state whether source/observer is moving and in which direction
Draw clear wave diagrams showing wavelength and amplitude when asked
Intensity questions often involve inverse square law - practice these calculations
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Sinusoidal wave showing wavelength λ (distance between crests), amplitude A (maximum displacement), and direction of energy transfer. Include labels for crest, trough, and equilibrium position.
